name: La Coasta Doble Trail
name_original: La Coasta Doble – dos bajadas clásicas desde Boltaña (ZZ-010)
description: Tackle the difficult La Coasta Doble Trail, a 15.3-mile mountain biking route in Zona Zero Pirineos with 3101 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Boltaña offers a diverse landscape within the Spanish Pyrenees, characterized by the Ara valley and surrounding mountains. The region features a network of historic trails, some of which have been recovered and maintained for mountain biking. Riders can expect varied terrain, from forest paths to more technical routes, with significant elevation changes across the area. Best mountain bike trails around Boltaña The most popular mountain bike trail is La Coasta Doble Trail, a 15.3 miles (24.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route features notable elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puente de los Moscarales – Ferrera Viewpoint loop from Boltaña, a moderate 16.5 miles (26.5 km) path. This loop offers scenic views and diverse riding surfaces. Local mountain bikers also love the Way to Guaso – Key Section loop from Boltaña, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Boltaña is defined by Pyrenean mountains, the Ara River valley, and a network of historic trails. The routes offer options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Boltaña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Boltaña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Boltaña?
A: Boltaña offers a wide selection of mountain bike routes, with over 20 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate loops to more challenging climbs, ensuring there's something for every mountain biker.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on mountain bike trails around Boltaña?
A: The terrain around Boltaña is diverse, ranging from forest paths to more technical routes, often featuring significant elevation changes. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971230616/la-coasta-doble-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coasta Doble Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route with notable elevation gain, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Moscarales – Ferrera Viewpoint loop from Boltaña&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate ride with scenic views and varied surfaces.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Boltaña?
A: While many trails in Boltaña are moderate to difficult, there are a couple of easier options available. These routes provide a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the region, allowing beginners to enjoy the scenic Pyrenean landscape without overly technical challenges. You can filter for 'easy' tours on komoot to find them.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Boltaña?
A: Yes, Boltaña features several excellent circular mountain bike routes. These loops are popular among local riders for their convenience and diverse scenery. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Moscarales – Ferrera Viewpoint loop from Boltaña&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Way to Guaso – Key Section loop from Boltaña&lt;/a&gt;, both offering varied riding experiences.

Q: What natural attractions or viewpoints can I see while mountain biking in Boltaña?
A: Boltaña is surrounded by stunning natural beauty. Many trails offer views of the Pyrenean mountains and the pristine Ara River valley. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3486403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Aínsa and Peña Montañesa from Partara Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also close to Ordesa and Monte Perdido National Park, known for its impressive mountains and deep gorges.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or places for wild swimming near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts several beautiful water features. The Ara River itself has natural pools, such as the Gorga natural pool in Boltaña, which is perfect for swimming. You can also find impressive waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6125681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aso River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1380725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saltador de las Lañas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible from nearby trails or short detours.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Boltaña?
A: Boltaña, located in the Spanish Pyrenees, generally offers excellent mountain biking conditions from spring through autumn. The summer months provide warm weather, while spring and autumn bring milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Always check local weather forecasts, especially for higher elevations, as conditions can change.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Boltaña?
A: The mountain bike trails in Boltaña are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning Pyrenean scenery, and the well-maintained network of historic trails that make for an engaging and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any historic sites or villages to explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. Boltaña itself has a picturesque old quarter and the ruins of a 10th-century castle. Nearby, the village of Aínsa is a historic-artistic site with medieval streets and a fortress. These locations offer a rich cultural experience alongside your mountain biking adventures, providing great opportunities for exploration off the bike.

Q: Are there options for mountain bike rental in Boltaña?
A: While specific rental shops are not detailed in the guide data, Boltaña is an internationally recognized mountain biking hub. It's advisable to check with local tourism offices or sports shops in Boltaña or nearby larger towns like Aínsa for mountain bike rental services to ensure you have the right equipment for your ride.

Q: How can I access the mountain bike trails in Boltaña, and is parking available?
A: Boltaña is accessible by road, and parking is generally available within the village, especially near trailheads or designated parking areas. For specific trail starting points, it's best to consult the individual route details on komoot, which often provide information on parking or access points. Public transport options to Boltaña might be limited, so driving is often the most convenient way to reach the area.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders in Boltaña?
A: Yes, Boltaña is known for its challenging routes suitable for experienced riders. Many trails feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971230616/la-coasta-doble-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coasta Doble Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31858569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferrera Viewpoint – Puente de los Moscarales loop from Boltaña&lt;/a&gt; are both rated as difficult, offering a demanding and rewarding experience for advanced mountain bikers.
tips: Old stone stepped bridge over the Ferrera ravine. It is a Romanesque masonry bridge of great height and light and for this reason it needs steps to bridge the difference in level.

This viewpoint does not get its name from looking at the Sierra Ferrera, since that is not where it looks. Its name is due to the ravine of the same name that you cross several times to reach it and you can see the Tres Serols and other peaks of the Monteperdido massif.
In this small town, with only 3 inhabitants, the Pyrenean Lucien Breit stayed once on his journey between Boltaña and Laguarta. It was September 9, 1907 and he slept at Casa Cabero.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike the La Coasta Doble Trail?
faqs.answer: The La Coasta Doble Trail is a challenging route that typically takes around 2 hours and 47 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult. Riders should expect significant elevation changes, with over 945 meters of ascent and 950 meters of descent. The terrain can be varied, often featuring rocky sections and technical singletrack that demand good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the La Coasta Doble Trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse and engaging terrain. The trail features a mix of surfaces, including rocky sections, potentially loose ground, and technical singletrack. It's designed to offer a challenging mountain biking experience with varied features throughout.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the La Coasta Doble Trail, you'll encounter several interesting points. Keep an eye out for the historic Puente de los Moscarales, a picturesque bridge. You'll also pass the Ferrera Viewpoint, offering great scenic vistas, and ride through the charming Campodarbe Village.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike in the Zona Zero Pirineos region, including this trail, is generally during the spring and autumn months. The weather is usually milder and more stable, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potential snow or colder conditions of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the La Coasta Doble Trail?
faqs.answer: The La Coasta Doble Trail typically starts and ends near Boltaña, as indicated by its route description. You should look for designated parking areas within or on the outskirts of Boltaña that cater to trail users. Specific parking instructions might be available on local Zona Zero Pirineos information boards or websites.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the La Coasta Doble Trail?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs on trails in Zona Zero Pirineos can vary. Generally, dogs are often permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. It's always best to check the most current local guidelines from Zona Zero Pirineos before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for mountain biking in Zona Zero Pirineos?
faqs.answer: Zona Zero Pirineos is a dedicated mountain biking destination. While there isn't typically an entrance fee for the overall park, some specific events or services might require payment. It's always a good idea to check the official Zona Zero Pirineos website for any current permit requirements or regulations before your visit, especially for organized activities.

faqs.question: Does the La Coasta Doble Trail connect with other routes in Zona Zero Pirineos?
faqs.answer: Yes, the La Coasta Doble Trail (ZZ010) is part of the extensive Zona Zero Pirineos network. While riding it, you will also traverse sections of other official trails, such as Sobrarbe. BTT. Ruta 2.1 Variante Pista de los Rojos. Campodarve – Boltaña, and Sobrarbe. BTT. Ruta 2. Por Boltaña y Morcat, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficult rating and duration, you should bring essential mountain biking gear. This includes a well-maintained mountain bike, a helmet, appropriate protective gear, plenty of water, energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), and a first-aid kit. It's also wise to carry a map or GPS device and a fully charged phone.

faqs.question: Is the La Coasta Doble Trail a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the La Coasta Doble Trail is designed as a loop route. It starts and finishes in the same general area, offering a complete circuit without the need for shuttling. The similar elevation gain and loss also indicate its loop nature.
